name: Birchwood Park
description: Birchwood Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Morton, IL. There are 6 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Cullinan Park
description: Cullinan Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Tremont, IL. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can’t reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Dee Mack High School
description: Dee Mack High School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Mackinaw, IL. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms and water.

name: Five Points
description: Five Points is a place to play pickleball in Washington, IL. There are 4 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to water.

name: Illinois Central College, CougarPlex
description: Come play pickleball at Illinois Central College, CougarPlex in East Peoria, IL! There are 4 courts. 2 are outdoors and 2 are indoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Parkside Fitness
description: Come play pickleball at Parkside Fitness in Pekin, IL! There are 18 acrylic and asphalt courts. 10 are outdoors and 8 are indoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like food, restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, locker rooms, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts cannot be reserved.
fees: Fee required — Members - Free, Guests - $5 drop-in, $45 punch card (10 visits)

name: Red Rock Bar & Grill
description: Red Rock Bar & Grill is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Morton, IL. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A one-time fee is required to play.
